sampleRecords.nature: Multiple severe wounds and internal injuries
sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle or object propelled by another vehicle in nonroadway area
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was helping as a forklift was getting a load unjammed in a trailer. The load shifted and crushed the employee, who suffered broken ribs and a punctured liver. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.employer: Customs and Border Protection-U.S. Border Patrol
sampleRecords.event_description: Nonroadway noncollision  fall or jump from moving vehicle
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On 7/31/2025, an employee was training to ride an ATV when it went into a draw and she went over the front of the ATV, landing on the ground. The employee sustained a fractured arm.

sampleRecords.employer: US. Department of Homeland Security - Immigration and Customs Enforcement
sampleRecords.part_of_body: Body systems and other part(s) of body
sampleRecords.event_description: Multiple types of overexertions, bodily motion and position
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was participating in special response team (SRT) tryouts and sustained rhabdomyolysis performing strenuous activities.

sampleRecords.employer: U.S. Customs and Border Protection - Corpus Christi Border Patrol Station
sampleRecords.nature: Multiple severe wounds and internal injuries
sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle in nonroadway area  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was struck by a vehicle and suffered a non-displaced fracture of the right hip, a fractured lower vertebra, fractured right pelvis, fractured toes, and a laceration to the left kidney.

sampleRecords.employer: U.S. Customs and Border Protection - Laredo Convent Avenue Port of Entry
sampleRecords.event_description: Exposure to harmful substance through exposed tissue  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer had been inspecting the trunk of a vehicle. He collapsed and lost vital signs due to possible fentanyl exposure. There were shards of glass found in the area the officer was inspecting that tested positive for traces of fentanyl and methamphetamines. The employee was hospitalized.
